references.name: Ableton
references.takeaway: Organize content as discrete modular rack-units—each block is a self-contained panel with its own header, content, and controls, tiled vertically like hardware modules mounted in a synthesizer rack.
applied: Each dashboard metric card is a self-contained rack unit with its own waveform canvas, value display, and delta indicator
Whiteboard synth modules follow the rack-unit pattern with individual headers, control bodies, and I/O jacks
Settings mixer channels are independent channel strips with their own labeled header bar and control sections
Each kanban card contains its own waveform thumbnail, metadata, and status — no shared state between cards
Signal flow blocks are standalone processing units with their own entity metadata and frequency annotations

references.name: Native Instruments
references.takeaway: Use a deep teal-on-midnight color scheme with a single hot accent (red or magenta) reserved only for active/live states, and set all type in a geometric sans-serif at 3 distinct weights to create the readout precision of a synth display.
applied: Primary palette uses deep indigo (#0c0820) as midnight void with electric cyan (#00e5ff) as teal-adjacent primary accent
Hot magenta (#ff00aa) reserved exclusively for active states: playing tracks, user messages, high-priority tags, and artist names
Syncopate heading font provides geometric sans-serif display precision at 2 weights (400, 700)
Fira Code monospace at regular and medium weights for technical readouts (BPM, keys, frequencies, timestamps)
Lexend body font at 4 weights (300-700) for hierarchical content text

references.name: Splice
references.takeaway: Render inline waveform graphics and audio-spectrum visualizations as decorative content elements, and use neon accent colors (cyan, magenta, electric purple) only for interactive states and progress indicators against a near-black (#121212) background.
applied: Dashboard metric cards feature inline animated waveform canvases as primary content imagery
Release roster tracks display miniature waveform previews in each row
Kanban cards include waveform thumbnail canvases as visual content elements
Vinyl vault records have waveform art on their sleeve covers
AI chat messages display voice-memo-style waveform visualizations
Sample library preview panel shows a large waveform canvas for the selected file
Dashboard spectrum chart renders a 64-bar animated frequency visualization
Neon cyan and magenta used exclusively for interactive states (hover, active, focus) and progress indicators (status badges, meter fills)

divergences: Background color is slightly darker (#0c0820) than Splice's #121212 to create a deeper void for plasma effects to stand out against
Unlike Native Instruments' use of red for alerts only, magenta is used more broadly as a secondary accent for artist identity and user-facing elements
Added animated canvas-based waveforms (not static SVG) to enhance the 'live electronic performance' feel beyond what the reference sites do with static imagery
